behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the directory apps/studio/data/database-queues/, the four operations (send/query/archive/delete), the literal() function from @supabase/pg-meta/src/pg-format, and behavioral examples of escaping that tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the vulnerability and mentions the available literal() helper but doesn't dictate exact line changes; the agent must identify and modify each interpolation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests capture SQL output and check that escaped forms (e.g., O''Reilly) appear — any correct use of literal() or equivalent proper escaping would pass, not specific variable names.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run tsx to execute the actual modified functions and capture SQL via a mock executeSql, then assert on runtime output.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub returning empty would fail _assert_value_escaped_in_sql which checks the doubled-quote escaped form appears in captured SQL.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover all four operations (send/query/archive/delete) and the literal() behaviors (string, object, null, number) described in the instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates / Fail-to-pass) and descriptive function names like test_literal_escapes_sql_injection, test_send_payload_escaped.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code modification, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and function names (sendDatabaseQueueMessage, getDatabaseQueue, etc.) are consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The domain area (database-queues) is part of the task description, which is appropriate; no specific line numbers are given.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not a config edit.
